title: Forged JWT grants /api/admin access
summary: A token signed with the weak lab secret and a tampered role claim is accepted by /api/admin, allowing privilege escalation to admin data.
why_it_matters: JWT forgery turns weak signing secrets and client-controlled role claims into a full server-side privilege escalation path.
manual_steps: Capture a normal user token or start from the known weak secret in the lab.
Forge a new JWT with role set to admin.
Send the forged token to GET /api/admin and confirm privileged access.
hint_ladder: The weak JWT setup is only the first half of the chain.
Try using the forged token against the admin-only API endpoint instead of the browser app.
The flag is earned when a forged admin token returns the protected /api/admin data.
what_to_learn: How weak secrets and trusted client claims combine into real privilege escalation.
How to move from token analysis to exploit validation.
Why server-side authorization must not depend only on client-signed role fields.
remediation: Use a strong random signing secret and rotate it safely.
Enforce authorization from server-side identity data, not only token role claims.
Reject forged or tampered tokens and monitor privileged endpoint access.
